series_performance_data.note: REAL performance data from Justin's LinkedIn — use this to understand what works

posts.title: The Wrong Villain — Original
posts.hook: Social media didn't create the mental health crisis. It amplified one that already existed.
posts.data_point: Stats showing social media as amplifier not originator

posts.title: Wrong Villain Part Two — The Villain Was Already In The Room
posts.hook: The screen was downstream of the poverty
posts.data_point: 23,155 — ten years of data. Poverty lowered life satisfaction. The extra scrolling did not.
posts.verdict: LOW PERFORMER — early post, low reach at time of posting. Narrative-led, less data-specific.

posts.title: Key Insight 03 — The Effect Size That Changes Everything
posts.hook: β = 0.061. 46 studies. 79 effect sizes. Below the evidentiary threshold. Statistically indistinguishable from noise.
posts.data_point: Child Poverty → Mental Health: 32%. Social Media → Mental Health: 3%. Four independent research groups. Same conclusion.
posts.verdict: BEST PERFORMER — THIS IS THE TEMPLATE. Specific beta coefficient + comparison format. Keep building posts exactly like this with NEW data.

posts.title: Key Insight 04 — The 2012 Claim. The 2008 Reality.
posts.hook: In UK data the mental health bar does not change gradient in 2012. The drivers were already in motion.
posts.data_point: Timeline: mental health crisis starts accelerating post-2008 financial crash, 4 years before Haidt's 2012 smartphone claim
posts.verdict: UNDERPERFORMED — timeline/narrative format without a sharp data point at the centre. The insight is valid but the format is wrong.

series_learnings: MOST IMPORTANT: The β = 0.061 format IS the series. Keep building it. Find new data points, new effect sizes, new comparisons — same structure every time.
The series is GROWING — 41K → 59K impressions. The audience is building. Every new post reaches more people than the last.
Do NOT avoid the effect size / social media mental health topic — that IS the Wrong Villain thesis. Go deeper into it with new data.
What NOT to repeat is the SPECIFIC FINDING (β = 0.061 from those 46 studies) — find the NEXT precise data point in the same space.
Comparison bar charts (Child Poverty 32% vs Social Media 3%) make the proportionality argument visual and shareable — use this format again.
Timeline posts without a data point at the centre underperform — always anchor to a number.
Effect size and meta-analysis data outperforms single study data — cite multiple studies and multiple research groups.
Four independent research groups reaching same conclusion is the strongest objection handler — always try to cite multiple sources.

series_posts_already_published.note: These specific posts are published. Do not re-post the same content. But DO continue the series with new data on the same themes.
published: The Wrong Villain — Original (amplifier thesis, social media as amplifier not originator)
The Villain Was Already In The Room (poverty data, 23,155 dataset)
Key Insight 03: The Effect Size That Changes Everything (β = 0.061, 46 studies, 79 effect sizes)
Key Insight 04: The 2012 Claim. The 2008 Reality. (2008 financial crash timeline)

next_series_directions: Find the next effect size — a new meta-analysis on a different driver (screen time vs content type, algorithm recommendation vs passive scrolling, age-specific effects). Same β format.
The funding gap: billions spent on device policy, pennies on conversation skills — find the actual spending figures and make the comparison.
Dumb phones fallacy — 84% still access internet via basic handsets. Precise data point, strong comparison, fits the format exactly.
Age-appropriate access: the actual science on what age unlocks what. Use official developmental psychology data, not policy guesses.
The algorithm accountability gap: what platforms know internally vs what they publish. Effect size format if data exists.
Screen time vs content type: same hours on TikTok vs YouTube vs messaging — wildly different outcomes. Find the comparative data.
objections_to_preempt: The average hides a vulnerable minority — address: concentrated harm is still harm. Wrong villain doesn't mean no villain. The 3% who are severely harmed deserve targeted intervention, not blunt device bans.
Missing citations — ALWAYS put source URL in FIRST COMMENT not post body. LinkedIn suppresses reach on posts with external links.
Social media as amplifier not originator — USE this as your core thesis. The screen is downstream of the deprivation.
Policy aimed at average child ignores the minority who are actually harmed — this is the strongest objection. Name it directly.
Effect size depends on the model used — cite multiple studies and meta-analyses, not single papers.
Corporate data gatekeeping — researchers can't access platform data to validate or refute claims.
Ban cuts lifeline for isolated kids — acknowledge this directly. The vulnerable minority uses social media for connection.
Haidt objection — his 2012 smartphone claim doesn't hold in UK data. Mental health acceleration began in 2008 post-financial crash.
voice_reminders: Proportionality spine in every post: what gets most attention vs what actually drives most impact
End every post with a question that invites the sharpest disagreement
NEVER put URLs in post body — LinkedIn suppresses reach. Use comment keyword CTA (e.g. Drop GUIDE in comments)
The proven format: precise data point → IS/ISN'T → memorable comparison → multiple independent sources → proportionality argument → series name → keyword CTA → repost ask → open question
Series is growing: 41K → 59K impressions. Keep going. The audience is compounding.
notes_to_self: The β = 0.061 post is the TEMPLATE to replicate with new data, not a topic to avoid. Find the next precise effect size or comparative data point in the children's digital safety space and write post #5 in the same format. The series compounds — keep building it.
